stages.expected_result: Operator can authenticate to the FPT Cloud web portal.
stages.validation_method: Manual browser validation; no safe API is available in this repository.

stages.expected_result: Dashboard and Compute Engine pages load in the web portal.
stages.validation_method: Manual browser validation.

stages.manual_check_item: Test FPT Cloud hotline / Call hotline 1900 638 399
stages.expected_result: Hotline is reachable and support workflow is available.
stages.validation_method: Manual phone call.

stages.manual_check_item: Resolve VPC ID from configured VPC name or provider lookup key
stages.expected_result: A real VPC ID is available from explicit HC_VPC_ID or from data.fptcloud_vpc.this.id.
stages.validation_method: Prefer explicit HC_VPC_ID as the effective VPC ID; otherwise query the official data.fptcloud_vpc data source by name using HC_VPC_NAME, VPC_NAME, VPC_ID, or VPC_IDS[0] as the configured lookup key.

stages.manual_check_item: Resolve storage policy required for VM/disk checks
stages.expected_result: A usable storage policy ID is available from HC_STORAGE_POLICY_ID or provider discovery.
stages.validation_method: Prefer explicit HC_STORAGE_POLICY_ID; otherwise query data.fptcloud_storage_policy.this.

stages.manual_check_item: Resolve existing subnet required for VM/security group checks
stages.expected_result: A usable subnet ID is available from HC_SUBNET_ID or provider discovery.
stages.validation_method: Prefer explicit HC_SUBNET_ID; otherwise query data.fptcloud_subnet.this.

stages.manual_check_item: Validate subnet creation inputs before provider apply
stages.expected_result: Subnet name, CIDR, gateway, VPC identifier shape, region, tenant, and Terraform module variables are locally valid or clearly reported as not API-verifiable.
stages.validation_method: Local dry-run validation only; no Terraform apply and no FPT Cloud resources are created.

stages.expected_result: A temporary VM subnet is created and reaches a ready state.
stages.validation_method: Run compute.validate-subnet-inputs first, then Terraform plan/apply using modules/subnet, then state/readiness inspection.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Always run terraform destroy for the stage workspace; destroy must be idempotent.

stages.manual_check_item: Collect subnet create evidence package for provider/API support
stages.expected_result: A sanitized evidence bundle is produced with effective config, provider lockfile info, module path, Terraform variables, validation warnings, plan output, Terraform logs, stage report, and latest create-subnet error classification.
stages.validation_method: Run local subnet validation, then Terraform init/plan/show only. Do not run Terraform apply by default.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Windows Server 2012 VM is created and visible in provider state.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module once image/flavor/key IDs are confirmed.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Destroy VM; retain only resources explicitly defined by the test.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Windows Server 2016 VM is created.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module once image/flavor/key IDs are confirmed.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Windows Server 2019 VM is created.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module once image/flavor/key IDs are confirmed.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Windows Server 2022 VM is created.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module once image/flavor/key IDs are confirmed.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Ubuntu 16.04 VM is created.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module once image/flavor/key IDs are confirmed.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Ubuntu 18.04 VM is created.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module once image/flavor/key IDs are confirmed.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Ubuntu 20.04 VM is created and reaches provider-ready state.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module and provider state inspection; guest OS access is not guaranteed for bare instances without network access.

stages.expected_result: Temporary Ubuntu 22.04 VM is created and reaches provider-ready state.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module and provider state inspection; guest OS access is not guaranteed for bare instances without network access.

stages.expected_result: VM is created with upsize flavor and reaches provider-ready state.
stages.validation_method: Terraform VM module with larger flavor; true in-place resize needs provider/API support.

stages.expected_result: OS disk capacity is expanded.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Manual cleanup if tested outside automation.

stages.manual_check_item: Add 40GB disk and attach to VM
stages.expected_result: A temporary 40GB disk is created; attach is attempted only when HC_INSTANCE_ID is supplied.
stages.validation_method: Terraform disk module and provider state inspection.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Always destroy disk resources created by the stage.

stages.manual_check_item: Delete VM and verify attached disk is retained
stages.expected_result: VM is deleted and attached data disk is retained.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.

stages.expected_result: Power schedule is created and executes.
stages.validation_method: No safe API/tool exists in this repository.

stages.expected_result: Snapshot is created.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.

stages.expected_result: VM is reverted from snapshot.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.

stages.expected_result: VM has public IP connectivity.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.

stages.manual_check_item: Create security group allowing only RDP 3389 and SSH 22
stages.expected_result: Temporary security group is created with SSH/RDP ingress rules.
stages.validation_method: Terraform security_group module and provider state inspection.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Always destroy security group resources created by the stage.

stages.expected_result: Ports outside SSH/RDP are blocked.
stages.validation_method: Manual or external network probe; no safe internal probe exists.

stages.expected_result: Outbound HTTP/HTTPS rules are present.
stages.validation_method: Terraform security_group module once rule semantics are confirmed.

stages.manual_check_item: Discover existing subnet inventory before additional subnet creation
stages.expected_result: Existing subnet names, IDs, CIDRs, gateways, and VPC IDs are collected when provider/API listing or HC_EXISTING_SUBNET_CIDRS is available.
stages.validation_method: Attempt to collect existing subnet CIDRs in the effective VPC. If provider/API subnet listing is available, collect subnet name, subnet ID, CIDR, gateway, and VPC ID. If listing is unavailable, use HC_EXISTING_SUBNET_CIDRS as operator-provided inventory. Do not guess subnet ranges.

stages.manual_check_item: Create additional subnet from configured non-overlapping CIDR
stages.expected_result: Temporary additional subnet is created.
stages.validation_method: Terraform subnet module with explicit HC_ADDITIONAL_SUBNET_CIDR and HC_ADDITIONAL_SUBNET_GATEWAY. Select the additional subnet CIDR using MAX_SUBNET_CANDIDATE_ATTEMPTS from spec constants. Start with HC_ADDITIONAL_SUBNET_CIDR, validate against discovered existing subnet CIDRs, append overlaps to rejected_cidrs with conflict_source=preflight_inventory, then deterministically increment to the next predictable network block of the same prefix length. Emit network.select-additional-subnet-cidr with candidate_attempt_count, rejected_cidrs, selected_cidr, and overlap_reason. Emit network.additional-subnet:attempt before each apply and network.additional-subnet:overlap-detected when provider error_code=804007 is returned. If Terraform apply fails with explicit subnet overlap error_code=804007, extract attempted CIDR and conflicting subnet name when available, append the attempted CIDR to the runtime conflict list with conflict_source=provider_error, destroy/cleanup any partial resources, select the next non-overlapping candidate, and retry. Stop when subnet creation succeeds or MAX_SUBNET_CANDIDATE_ATTEMPTS is reached. Do not retry unknown errors or provider/backend system errors. If the limit is reached, skip/stop Terraform apply with subnet_cidr_exhausted and report all attempted CIDRs.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Always run terraform destroy for the stage workspace.

stages.manual_check_item: Add additional NIC to VM and verify inside OS
stages.expected_result: VM has second NIC visible inside guest OS.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.

stages.manual_check_item: Create test file, create VM backup, run backup job, delete file, restore VM, verify file
stages.expected_result: Restored VM contains the test file.
stages.validation_method: No safe repository implementation exists.

stages.manual_check_item: Create bucket, upload file, create folder, open file, connect S3 endpoint, delete file, delete bucket
stages.expected_result: Temporary bucket is created in an enabled region and destroyed.
stages.validation_method: Terraform object_storage module; browser and S3 Browser checks remain manual.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Always destroy object storage resources created by the stage.

stages.manual_check_item: Create support ticket on support.fptcloud.com, update ticket status, add public comment
stages.expected_result: Support ticket workflow works.
stages.validation_method: Manual browser validation; no safe service desk API is configured.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Close or annotate manual test ticket.

stages.manual_check_item: Request support from Zalo OA using /support
stages.expected_result: Zalo OA support workflow responds.
stages.validation_method: Manual mobile or Zalo client validation.

stages.manual_check_item: Create ticket by sending email to support@fptcloud.com
stages.expected_result: Inbound support email creates or updates a ticket.
stages.validation_method: Manual email validation; repository has no outbound mail harness.
stages.cleanup_behavior: Close or annotate manual test ticket.
